intro: Myrsine rockii, also known as Korean myrsine, is a plant species in the Myrsinaceae family, native to the Korean Peninsula and parts of China. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its reported health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Myrsine rockii include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, such as myrsinicine and myrsinoside, which have been identified as potential contributors to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Myrsine rockii is often prepared as a decoction or an extract, and is commonly used in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
